120417.explanation: We give farmers space to undertake business, but not always and everywhere. Secondary activities must fit the area, zoning plan, and surroundings. Custom solutions are needed to prevent nuisance and preserve the quality of the rural area.

120419.explanation: We are not in favor of a general ban on distribution centers. New establishments should be assessed individually based on location, traffic, landscape, and added value. Where it fits and creates employment, space should remain.

120420.explanation: Employers are responsible for good housing for seasonal workers. Housing on or near one's own company prevents extra pressure on residential neighborhoods and the housing market, provided it is neat, temporary, and meets clear quality standards.

120424.explanation: The municipality does not need to organize fireworks shows. New Year's Eve is primarily a celebration for residents themselves. We prefer to focus on safety and enforcement rather than on extra costs and organization by the municipality. We do support local initiatives.

120425.explanation: Free bulky waste collection at home sounds appealing but leads to higher costs for everyone. Residents can already dispose of bulky waste through existing facilities. We stick to personal responsibility and an affordable waste policy.

120428.explanation: We are open to custom solutions but not to a general right to build houses on agricultural land. Housing must fit within spatial policy, landscape, and surroundings. We make a careful assessment per location.

120429.explanation: Sports and exercise are important, but the municipality does not have to pay membership fees by default. There are already arrangements to support people with low incomes. We provide targeted help where needed, without new general obligations.

120430.explanation: A local broadcaster plays an important role, but extra funding is not automatic. We first look at quality, reach, and efficiency. Support is possible, but only if it clearly contributes to good local information provision.

120436.explanation: Visible enforcement contributes to safety and livability. More enforcement officers ensure faster action against nuisance and better cooperation with the police. This increases the feeling of safety and makes rules enforceable.

120437.explanation: Targeted camera surveillance can contribute to safety and preventing nuisance. We deploy it in places where it is truly needed, supporting enforcement and investigation, with clear agreements on privacy and proportionality.

120438.explanation: Safety for victims of domestic violence is paramount. A shelter does not necessarily have to be located in Noordwijk, as long as there is sufficient and well-accessible regional shelter and victims are helped quickly and safely.

120442.explanation: Holiday homes are intended for recreation and tourism. Permanent residence leads to fragmentation, pressure on facilities, and less control over quality. We focus on full-fledged housing within the cores, not living in holiday parks.

120443.explanation: We find it important that people with social assistance benefits, where possible, do socially meaningful work. Participation increases self-worth and opportunities. This always happens according to ability and with consideration for personal circumstances.

120445.explanation: We focus aid on residents who really need it. Further raising the income limit makes arrangements too broad and reduces the difference between working and not working. Targeted support and perspective on progress are paramount.

120447.explanation: More charging points are needed to enable electric driving. We expand this selectively, aligned with demand and without unnecessarily increasing parking pressure. This keeps it practical and feasible for residents.

120448.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount, but a general 30 km/h rule does not fit everywhere. We choose custom solutions: 30 km/h where it truly improves safety, and higher speeds where roads are suited for it.

120449.explanation: Choice in healthcare is important, also where religion or culture plays a role. At the same time, care must remain available, affordable, and of good quality. We strive for appropriate care but cannot provide absolute guarantees.

120451.explanation: The property tax can be lowered. Residents and entrepreneurs already face high costs. Where financially responsible, we want to reduce the tax burden to keep living and doing business in Noordwijk affordable.

120454.explanation: Business parks may expand where necessary and appropriate. This preserves space for local entrepreneurs, employment, and economic growth. Expansion happens carefully, with attention to accessibility, surroundings, and landscape.

120456.explanation: We are against giving priority to status holders for social housing. The scarce homes are primarily intended for residents of our own municipality. Equal opportunities and local ties must be leading principles in housing allocation.

120459.explanation: Swimming lessons are important, but responsibility lies with parents, not the municipality. For families needing help, good arrangements are already in place. This keeps swimming lessons accessible without extra costs for all residents.

120461.explanation: A fixed minimum of 35% social housing is not feasible everywhere. We adhere to the current standard of 30% and make a case-by-case assessment per location. This way, projects can proceed and we build for all target groups, not just one segment.

120462.explanation: More high-rise buildings are not a goal in themselves. Where taller residential buildings fit the environment and contribute to housing and quality, it can be considered. At the same time, we protect the village character and carefully evaluate each location.

120464.explanation: We are against raising parking fees in Noordwijk aan Zee. Parking must remain affordable for residents and visitors. We prefer smart solutions and spreading demand rather than higher rates that harm accessibility.

120466.explanation: We oppose the establishment of a (temporary) asylum seekers center on Achterweg. Housing construction must not be linked to asylum reception. The limited space and homes in Noordwijk are meant for our own residents, not for additional reception locations.

120467.explanation: Bulb fields are essential to the landscape, economy, and identity of Noordwijk. Housing should be located in the centers and designated spots. Therefore, we want to actively oppose housing on bulb fields and protect this land.

120468.explanation: More parking space around the Boulevard is needed to reduce parking pressure and illegal parking. Preferably underground or cleverly integrated, so that the appearance of the Boulevard improves and the area remains easily accessible for residents and visitors.

120469.explanation: A regional heating network can help phase out gas, provided it is reliable, affordable, and future-proof. We are positive about such a network when it offers a good alternative for residents and businesses without coercion or unnecessary risks.

120470.explanation: Yes, beach pavilions should be allowed to remain open all year. This strengthens the quality of the beach, creates employment, and makes Noordwijk attractive year-round, as long as agreements on appearance, nuisance, and safety are properly observed.

120417.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes tailored solutions are needed here. We support every farmer selling their products at home through a shop, but do not want campgrounds to spring up everywhere. It must be logically integrable with a clear plan.

120419.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes there is no extra space for large distribution centers in our municipality. Our residents benefit little from these as migrant workers do the labor. We prefer to give room to small and medium-sized enterprises that create jobs for residents.

120420.explanation: The Party for the Residents welcomes initiatives where migrant workers are housed at the companies they work for. Many migrant workers currently live in homes in our village centers, which we believe should become available for the regular market and our residents.

120424.explanation: From next year a complete fireworks ban will apply. We do not support this and believe residents should have the freedom to set off fireworks. As an alternative, we see a municipality-organized fireworks show as an appropriate solution.

120425.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes residents should, in principle, be responsible for taking their bulky waste to the recycling center. However, for elderly and residents without a car, a collection scheme should be arranged.

120428.explanation: The Party for the Residents finds it important to preserve the unique open bulb field area of our region. We will make clear agreements about this. Building freely on every plot threatens this landscape and the quality of life for residents and tourism.

120429.explanation: Every child must be able to play sports. We do not support income-based politics but prefer to support clubs that ensure sports remain accessible to everyone.

120430.explanation: The Party for the Residents values information provision from local public broadcasters. However, we believe the broadcasters already receive sufficient resources to perform their tasks well.

120436.explanation: The Party for the Residents considers safety essential for residents. We believe having more police visible on the streets is the most effective way to ensure this safety.

120437.explanation: The Party for the Residents finds safety essential. At risk locations, we want to expand camera surveillance. In cases of nuisance, we opt for a strict zero-tolerance policy to protect residents.

120438.explanation: The Party for the Residents condemns all forms of domestic violence. We believe the police must arrive quickly to stop the violence and support the victim. Together, appropriate shelter is arranged with acquaintances, friends, or family.

120442.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes living on holiday parks should be possible given the current housing shortage. This should be person-specific so that homes can revert to holiday accommodations when the housing shortage is resolved.

120443.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes all residents should contribute to our society. Socially meaningful work is a suitable solution. We focus on guiding people back to the regular labor market.

120445.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes the current income threshold is sufficient and does not need adjustment. We consider this threshold appropriate and fair for determining the support residents can receive.

120447.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes the municipality must closely monitor residents’ use of charging stations and electric cars. Based on this, it can be determined how many charging stations are needed in our municipality.

120448.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes speeds within residential neighborhoods should be limited to a maximum of 30 km/h. On safe main roads within neighborhoods, a speed of 50 km/h should be possible.

120449.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes every resident must accept what is offered. We trust the expertise of care providers regardless of religion, belief, or culture.

120451.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes residents should receive a 10% discount on the property tax (ozb). We want to finance this by cutting costs in the administrative apparatus.

120454.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes the Gravendam business park may be expanded to benefit employment and the business climate in our municipality.

120456.explanation: The Party for the Residents finds it unacceptable that a 20-year-old status holder gets priority for a social rental home with free furnishing, while a 30-year-old Noordwijk resident who has been on the waiting list for 12 years still lives in an attic room.

120459.explanation: We support the reintroduction of school swimming lessons. We believe the municipality should contribute financially to this.

120461.explanation: The Party for the Residents finds social rental housing important, but an excessively large share often jeopardizes the financial feasibility of projects. Affordable owner-occupied and mid-priced rental homes are also needed for residents who do not qualify for social rental housing.

120462.explanation: The Party for the Residents sees increasing building height as a possible solution to tackle the housing shortage. It is important this happens within clear frameworks, not in existing residential neighborhoods, but in new developments on the edges of our villages, such as along the N206. For our villages, this would be an exception to the rule.

120464.explanation: The Party for the Residents wants parking to be free for all residents of our municipality.

120466.explanation: The Party for the Residents opposes having an asylum seekers center in the municipality due to major concerns about safety, nuisance, crime, and pressure on facilities. Refugees should be helped through a centralized national approach or local shelter in their own region.

120467.explanation: The beautiful bulb fields are part of our identity. We must cherish this. At the same time, there is a significant housing challenge. We want to consult with the bulb cultivation sector about locations suitable for housing development.

120468.explanation: The Party for the Residents believes the municipality should invest more in realizing underground parking facilities to meet parking demand in Noordwijk aan Zee, especially along the Boulevard.

120469.explanation: The Party for the Residents finds heating networks too risky at this time. Costs are high or unclear, you are tied to one supplier, and the infrastructure is complex and expensive. Also, much freedom of choice for residents is lost.

120470.explanation: As the Party for the Residents, we want to maintain the current number of permanent beach pavilions. We do not want to allow additional permanent beach pavilions to protect the hospitality industry on the boulevard and in the village center.

120417.explanation: A shop or farm campground cannot be everywhere. Together we look at what is possible for sustainable agricultural businesses and what is fitting for Noordwijk.

120419.explanation: In recent years, far too many huge distribution centers have been built in the Netherlands. They spoil the landscape and much of the work is done by poorly paid labor migrants. We want to stop more distribution centers from arriving. This also protects our small retailers.

120420.explanation: Employers can certainly contribute to good housing for seasonal workers because they directly benefit from it. But the municipality must retain control. By placing management with housing corporations – as with other forms of housing – quality is better guaranteed.

120424.explanation: The best New Year's party is celebrated together with everyone, with one big laser or drone show organized by the municipality. This keeps it safe and allows us to enjoy a pleasant New Year's Eve together.

120425.explanation: We already pay €500 per year per household for waste collection. These costs should not be increased further.

120428.explanation: The rural area of Noordwijk must remain open. Housing construction is only permitted in the immediate vicinity of residential centers.

120429.explanation: Sports provide energy, fun, and keep you healthy. But for people with low incomes, joining a sports club is often too expensive. By extending minimum schemes to 150% of the social assistance standard, everyone can participate.

120430.explanation: Local media are incredibly important to know what is happening in your own environment, from the street to the municipal council. But many local media struggle to survive. This is a role for the municipality, both financially and supportively.

120436.explanation: The municipality already deploys extra manpower by structurally using the Suswacht on the Grent. Digitalization of parking management is also giving municipal enforcement officers more time for other tasks.

120437.explanation: There are already many cameras in the streets, including near houses and businesses. Extra municipal camera surveillance is very expensive but provides very little additional safety while limiting our privacy. We place extra cameras only where it is truly unsafe.

120438.explanation: If you no longer feel safe at home, there must be a place you can always go to. A women's shelter is indispensable in our region.

120442.explanation: There are 1,000-1,400 holiday homes in Noordwijk, of which over 500 are permanently inhabited. For urgent seekers, this is a temporary solution. Quickly building 1,000 social homes is the real solution.

120443.explanation: People need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. We encourage people receiving social assistance to become socially active and find their place in society.

120445.explanation: By extending minimum schemes to 150% of the social assistance standard, we also support low-middle incomes. This enables all children to participate in sports and school activities.

120447.explanation: There are increasing numbers of electric cars, but not everyone can charge at home. Therefore, more public charging stations are needed. Noordwijk currently has 337 public charging points but will need many more in the future.

120448.explanation: Lowering the maximum speed makes traffic in our municipality much safer. For cycling children and elderly people with mobility issues, this is a world of difference. Moreover, the streets become quieter and the air cleaner.

120449.explanation: We appreciate all healthcare providers, and they do their work for everyone regardless of faith, culture, or belief.

120451.explanation: We prevent increases in sewer and waste charges. To achieve this, we raise taxes on second homes (commuter tax), tourist tax, and parking by day visitors. The property tax may increase in line with inflation.

120454.explanation: Compared to the surroundings, Noordwijk already has relatively many business parks (Klei Oost, Gravendam, 's-Gravendijck, Delfweg-Bollenstreek Business Park). ESA/ESTEC is also expanding. The open landscape is under great pressure, so further expansion is undesirable for us.

120456.explanation: People with all forms of urgency, such as urgent seekers and the elderly, are given priority for a social rental home. This also includes refugees with a residence permit.

120459.explanation: Every child in Noordwijk, with its abundant open water, must learn to swim! School swimming lessons will return and we will make this easy for schools. The Binnenzee and De Schelft pools will be well used, and children will learn to swim safely and proficiently.

120461.explanation: We aim for 40% affordable homes for youth, starters, and the elderly. Care and education personnel also deserve affordable rental or owner-occupied housing in their own village. Noordwijk is significantly behind the Bollenstreek; there is a need for affordable housing.

120462.explanation: We have a huge housing shortage and action is needed. High-rise buildings allow for rapid construction of many homes. Build higher so we make good use of scarce space.

120464.explanation: Paid parking along the boulevard currently costs only €1 for the first two hours. By increasing parking rates here, we can make free parking available outside the village, with a free beach shuttle bus and rental bikes.

120466.explanation: By adopting the dispersion law, Noordwijk is obligated to contribute to the reception of refugees. Where this will be is discussed with residents, the municipal council, and the Central Agency for the Reception of Asylum Seekers (COA).

120467.explanation: Bulb fields are beautiful, but the housing shortage is too great. We build on the edges of existing residential centers and only limited on bulb fields. Groot-Bronsgeest is a good example.

120468.explanation: More parking spaces around the Boulevard increase traffic chaos on beach days, while they remain empty for much of the year. Only if Wilhelmina Boulevard becomes car-free does parking elsewhere add value.

120469.explanation: Comfortably heating our homes becomes cheaper for everyone if we smartly collaborate on a heat network. Sustainable and social!

120470.explanation: There are eight permanent beach pavilions along our coast, which is enough. They provide the right balance between nature and economy. Expansion is not necessary. We are Noordwijk, not Zandvoort!

120417.explanation: If it is small-scale and there is demand for it, that should be allowed.

120419.explanation: If there is demand and on industrial estates, that should be possible. It also creates employment.

120420.explanation: If possible, this would certainly be preferred. It ensures that ordinary homes become available again for local residents.

120424.explanation: Then everyone comes together there and anyone can meet. However, this does cost money.

120425.explanation: On request, so less waste ends up on the street and in nature.

120428.explanation: There are already many houses in the rural area and the number of farms is decreasing, so these can use the existing homes in the rural area.

120429.explanation: A limited amount, as is already arranged now. Sports must be possible for everyone.

120430.explanation: That is not necessary; the existing subsidy should be sufficient.

120436.explanation: Safety is very important and enforcement is necessary.

120437.explanation: To create a feeling of safety, images should only be used if necessary.

120438.explanation: There is one in Leiden with which we closely collaborate.

120442.explanation: Because the housing shortage is currently high, that could be temporarily allowed.

120443.explanation: This way, you stay among the people.

120445.explanation: Work and self-reliance must be encouraged.

120447.explanation: There are now enough, and this comes at the expense of free public parking spaces.

120448.explanation: That is not necessary for all roads within built-up areas, only where it is needed for the safety of pedestrians and cyclists.

120451.explanation: Owners of more expensive houses pay more, and that is fair.

120456.explanation: The housing market is already overcrowded; our own residents hardly get a chance anymore.

120459.explanation: Swimming in this water-rich area is a basic necessity.

120461.explanation: At the current rate of 30%, it remains affordable to build this way and sufficient social rental homes are constructed.

120462.explanation: Not too high, but it may be somewhat higher to realize more homes.

120466.explanation: There are not enough facilities for that.

120467.explanation: This should be considered on a case-by-case basis.

120468.explanation: The boulevard must become low-traffic for cars.

120469.explanation: Look at what is safely possible and keep costs as limited as possible.

120470.explanation: Not necessary and unfair competition for the hospitality industry on the boulevard.

120417.explanation: Yes, but it should not dominate over normal business activities.

120419.explanation: Does not fit with our ambitions. We prefer to use the available space to build affordable homes for our own residents.

120420.explanation: If possible, this is our preference.

120424.explanation: We support preserving Dutch traditions as much as possible.

120425.explanation: We believe this should be a service to prevent bulky waste from being dumped.

120429.explanation: Everyone should be able to participate.

120430.explanation: Having a local broadcaster is important and must continue to exist.

120436.explanation: Society is becoming harsher. We support more police presence on the streets and a police station returning to Noordwijk.

120437.explanation: More camera surveillance is needed in the nightlife areas.

120438.explanation: It is important that there is a facility for this.

120442.explanation: Tourism and recreation are an important pillar for our municipality. We especially want to realize more affordable housing.

120443.explanation: We support people continuing to participate in society.

120445.explanation: We see the current 120% of the minimum income as a good threshold.

120447.explanation: We must keep up with the increase in the number of electric cars. This number is currently rising.

120448.explanation: Traffic safety is paramount. A speed limit of 30 km/h in built-up areas is appropriate.

120451.explanation: If possible, for both residents and entrepreneurs.

120454.explanation: Entrepreneurs are important for the municipality's economy. We must make room for them.

120456.explanation: We give maximum priority to our own residents.

120459.explanation: Noordwijk is a water-rich municipality. Every child must learn to swim. We promote this through school swimming.

120461.explanation: In Noordwijk, 30% of homes are social housing. We prefer a higher percentage of affordable owner-occupied homes, so also above the social threshold.

120462.explanation: We have limited space to build. To use the space optimally, we must partially build upwards.

120464.explanation: We use parking fees to regulate, besides that, in our view, they should be cost-covering.

120466.explanation: We do not see that location as suitable given the previously stated numbers. For us, support from residents is the most important factor.

120467.explanation: We are fundamentally against housing development on bulb-growing land. But because the need for homes among our own residents is high, we do want to allow housing near the village centers.

120468.explanation: We need to create more parking spaces as needed. However, we want this around the Boulevard, not on the Boulevard.

120469.explanation: We support a sustainable heating network provided it is affordable. More research on this is still needed.

120470.explanation: Accessibility and tranquility on the beach are important to us. We do not think the entire beach should be commercially exploited.

120419.explanation: This does not fit within a healthy living environment as Noordwijk wants to be.

120420.explanation: Not must, but may. For employers who want this, we must create the opportunity.

120424.explanation: A central show can help reinforce the fireworks ban.

120428.explanation: We follow the rules agreed upon in Noordwijk. This way we keep control over the landscape and undesirable developments.

120429.explanation: We maintain existing schemes so that children from less wealthy parents can continue to play sports.

120430.explanation: Sufficient funds are currently allocated to the local broadcaster BO.

120436.explanation: There are enough enforcement officers at the moment. However, we want more community police officers on the streets and to meet the ratio of 1 officer per 5,000 residents.

120437.explanation: There are enough cameras in high-risk areas. Also, through 'camera in view' the police can use private cameras when necessary.

120442.explanation: There is currently a shortage of holiday homes and these are not suitable for permanent residence. We want to make an exception for urgent seekers as a temporary solution.

120443.explanation: Not only focus on obligations but especially stimulate paid work to eventually help someone out of welfare.

120445.explanation: The current threshold of 130% and the additional support, both financial and physical, are sufficient to ensure no one is left out in the cold.

120447.explanation: There are currently enough charging spots. If this becomes problematic, we should consider more centralized charging locations.

120449.explanation: There are already too few healthcare providers. If people can choose who helps them or not, the system will become even more strained.

120451.explanation: That is not a goal in itself, but if housing values increase, we lower the property tax percentage. In 2026, the percentage was reduced by more than 10%.

120454.explanation: We have already decided that in Noordwijkerhout.

120456.explanation: This is determined nationally. We await the new legislation. This 'abolishment' means the 96-month waiting period is shortened by 2 months. More affordable housing solves many problems.

120459.explanation: PUUR wants all children in Noordwijk to be able to swim. We have two swimming pools; make them available for swimming schools so municipal costs remain low. Free swimming for children who cannot swim starting from group 3. Parents pay for this themselves.

120461.explanation: We adhere to the housing vision of 30% social, 40% mid-range, and 30% private sector. This keeps building affordable for developers. More than 30% is of course allowed!

120462.explanation: At the edges of villages, high-rise buildings should be possible for new construction, but with affordable homes such as mid-range rental and ownership. Due to scarcity of land and high prices, affordable building is only possible by building upwards.

120464.explanation: This has already become considerably more expensive from 2026. We only choose this as a last resort, when difficult choices must be made. For residents, we keep the cheap parking cards.

120466.explanation: PUUR opposes large-scale refugee accommodation. If legally required, then small-scale housing in all villages, under clear conditions such as safety, affordability, and fitting the environment.

120467.explanation: We will also have to build in the rural areas to provide enough homes for our own residents. This cannot be done only within urban areas.

120468.explanation: PUUR wants to make the Boulevard car-free. That costs parking spaces. Also, new developments reduce public parking spaces. To keep the shopping area healthy, parking must be possible there.

120469.explanation: We await current studies before saying yes or no. Condition: it must be affordable for residents and the municipality must bear no financial risk. A heating network can help solve our electricity problem.

120470.explanation: The current permanent pavilions meet year-round demand. If we also keep temporary pavilions open in winter, it will harm the existing entrepreneurs on the beach and Boulevard.

120417.explanation: We welcome business initiatives that contribute to enriching our villages.

120419.explanation: The establishment of distribution centers helps reduce traffic congestion in the neighborhoods.

120420.explanation: This frees up homes in neighborhoods for our own residents. It also prevents more migrant workers living here than working here.

120424.explanation: This is a good alternative to the fireworks ban.

120425.explanation: This prevents the litter we see too often at environmental parks now.

120428.explanation: This way, a part of the housing shortage is resolved.

120429.explanation: Sports is a healthy and social activity. It must be possible for everyone.

120430.explanation: The statutory contribution is sufficient to function well.

120436.explanation: There are enough enforcement officers. They should be more visible in neighborhoods and focus more on safety than on enforcement perceived as annoying.

120437.explanation: This can help in places where residents feel unsafe.

120438.explanation: Agreements have been made in the region that the central municipality Leiden provides this.

120442.explanation: Holiday homes belong to our seaside town and strengthen the economy. In cases where people already live there permanently, we tolerate the current use. Labor migrants DO NOT fall under this regulation.

120443.explanation: We maintain the existing reciprocal obligation based on ability, where you do something in return for your benefit.

120445.explanation: Any increase must not result in work no longer being rewarding.

120447.explanation: When installing charging stations, we always consult with local residents. After charging, a charging spot must be freed up. We want to prevent parking from becoming more difficult in residential neighborhoods for residents with petrol cars.

120448.explanation: Within the built-up area, a maximum speed of 50 km/h contributes to traffic flow and safety. In residential neighborhoods, 30 km/h is possible.

120449.explanation: One can use the offerings that are already accessible to everyone.

120451.explanation: This can be reduced, among other things, by critically reviewing the civil service and limiting external hiring.

120454.explanation: This contributes to our local economy and employment.

120456.explanation: The housing shortage is already big enough and Lijst Salman Noordwijk chooses its own residents.

120459.explanation: This money is already available for residents with a small budget.

120461.explanation: By building 30% social rental housing, we achieve our goal and ensure a varied housing supply.

120462.explanation: This can help reduce the housing shortage. However, it cannot be done everywhere!

120464.explanation: The price of the day ticket for the larger parking lots may be increased.

120466.explanation: Lijst Salman Noordwijk grants no cooperation whatsoever and never to the establishment of an asylum seekers center (azc) in our municipality, neither temporary nor permanent, neither large nor small. Not in Noordwijk, not in Noordwijkerhout, and not in De Zilk.

120467.explanation: Our residents have the right to housing!

120468.explanation: The operation of this must be financially viable.

120469.explanation: Provided that heating remains affordable for residents.

120417.explanation: Permission for farmers to conduct additional activities alongside farming always requires a tailored approach. Permission depends on the type of business, the plans, and the environment in which the company is located. Our basic attitude is positive.

120419.explanation: D66 Noordwijk wants economic activities with high added value in Noordwijk. We do not support a distribution center that takes up our scarce space and creates jobs that require even more low-paid labor migrants.

120420.explanation: Labor migrants are currently urgently needed in our region. We prefer housing for labor migrants to be separate from the employer to prevent dependency and abuse. In certain cases, an exception might be possible for seasonal work.

120424.explanation: A fireworks show is expensive, environmentally harmful, and unpleasant for pets and some residents. A private initiative can be considered by the municipality, but our public funds can be spent better.

120425.explanation: Households should be able to have bulky waste collected at home for free once a year. This prevents residents from simply placing bulky waste on the roadside or dumping it somewhere. For low-income households and those who cannot visit the recycling center, this removes a barrier.

120428.explanation: We support the development of housing in specific locations, such as Bronsgeest and Vinkeveld-South, but we are also careful with the prime bulb fields available in our region. Additionally, we protect the unique character of our landscape. Unrestricted self-building does not fit with this.

120429.explanation: The Youth Fund Sport & Culture is already available in Noordwijk to pay for memberships for children. People receiving social assistance must have sufficient means to pay for a membership. We do not prefer an additional separate scheme.

120430.explanation: D66 considers local journalism highly valuable for the functioning of (local) democracy. Local media contribute to better information provision for residents and can explain politics. Additional municipal support is needed as a supplement to national funding.

120436.explanation: Enforcers must be deployed where needed (against nuisance from odor, smoke, waste, dog waste, and intimidation) and with attention to new threats such as trick scams or internet fraud. With proper reporting, we then address the underlying causes.

120437.explanation: More camera surveillance is usually not the solution to existing problems. D66 Noordwijk prefers to tackle the causes. Additionally, the costs are high, effectiveness is limited, and privacy concerns exist. Only in exceptional cases is it an option for us.

120438.explanation: D66 Noordwijk wants to ensure regionally sufficient places in women's shelters and support for women and children affected. The municipality plays an important role in signaling, preventing, and addressing domestic violence and must develop a coherent and effective approach.

120442.explanation: We are not in favor of permanent living at holiday parks. In times of housing shortage, we do see possibilities for holiday homes as temporary bridging or for emergency situations. This is in line with national regulations.

120443.explanation: We use the possibilities within the Participation Act to activate people. Everyone should be able to participate and people need help with that. We also realize that for some residents it is genuinely impossible or not the right time. We must show understanding for that.

120445.explanation: According to the Nibud, people with low incomes are increasingly able to make ends meet, at least if they use all available support. People need help with debts and applying for allowances and poverty schemes, among others. Give a fishing rod, not a fish. D66 Noordwijk focuses on this.

120447.explanation: D66 Noordwijk wants more opportunities for charging on streets and in the neighborhood, with transparent pricing by providers. More and more people in our municipality drive electrically and this will only increase. Electric charging must be available and transparent for everyone.

120448.explanation: The most vulnerable road users deserve the most protection. D66 Noordwijk places this perspective at the center when it comes to traffic safety in our village. This means standard 30 km/h zones and even walking pace near schools. Some main roads are exempted for good traffic flow.

120449.explanation: All healthcare must take into account religion, culture, or belief. As liberals, we do not distinguish in this. Specialization by care providers is, of course, possible.

120451.explanation: Especially owners of many properties benefit from this. D66 wants to keep the burdens for all residents as low as possible. We are not against an increase in the property tax equal to inflation. Property tax is a small part of taxes in the Netherlands and pays for valuable things in our village.

120454.explanation: We focus on qualitative growth of business parks. Sustainable, vital business parks with high-quality employment where entrepreneurs strengthen each other. We facilitate sharing of raw materials and waste streams and invest in infrastructure for reuse and recycling.

120456.explanation: We adhere to the national and provincial task to shelter these vulnerable people. We are in solidarity with other municipalities to solve asylum reception problems together and relieve the asylum chain.

120459.explanation: In a seaside village, every child should be able to swim when leaving primary school. Especially since we also have two swimming pools. This way, the swimming culture in our village receives an extra boost. The practical implementation may be difficult and it should not be an extra burden for education.

120461.explanation: Currently, less than 30% social rental housing is available in Noordwijk. To reach this percentage in the future, we will temporarily need to build more than 30% social rental housing at locations where this is possible.

120462.explanation: We want to preserve the village’s small-scale character with a maximum height of about 5 floors. Infilling and adding extra floors to apartments are options to create more housing.

120464.explanation: We advocate considering separate summer and winter parking rates. Parking garages can be more expensive than street parking. Above all, we want to invest in good accessibility by public transport and shared bikes, so fewer cars drive to Zee.

120466.explanation: We accommodate people in asylum seeker centers in accordance with the distribution law. We take residents' concerns seriously but realize that reception generally goes well in most parts of the Netherlands. Achterweg is one of the options for an asylum center, but this needs further investigation first.

120467.explanation: We support the development of housing in specific locations such as Bronsgeest and Vinkeveld-South but are also careful with the prime bulb cultivation land available in our region and the unique character of our landscape.

120468.explanation: People coming from outside Noordwijk should park as much as possible in parking garages or travel to our village by public transport. We advocate for the largest and deepest possible parking garages under Palaceplein, Vuurtorenplein, and Wantveld.

120469.explanation: D66 supports the development of sustainable heating networks. Combined with geothermal energy, there are opportunities to meet climate goals and preserve our landscape. Our conditions are: 1) it is financially the best option, 2) technically undisputed, and 3) not mandatory so that people can choose.

120470.explanation: We are not in favor of additional permanent beach pavilions. These come at the expense of other Noordwijk hospitality businesses during the quiet winter period. They also create a busier image of Noordwijk, while in the low season we want to attract people as a peaceful destination.

120417.explanation: HELDER wants to give farmers space for secondary activities, but not without conditions. Every application must be assessed for spatial planning, support, and possible side effects such as housing for migrant workers. Not everything can be done everywhere and at any time.

120419.explanation: HELDER assesses initiatives for distribution centers based on their added value for employment, economy, and spatial quality. We are critical of large distribution halls without social utility but do not believe the municipality should block all new establishments.

120420.explanation: HELDER believes agricultural employers should be involved in solving the housing issue for migrant workers but this should not become a unilateral obligation. Housing on their own premises is not possible or desirable everywhere and requires customization and cooperation.

120424.explanation: HELDER promotes art and culture and wants more events for young people. Together with local entrepreneurs, New Year's Eve should be a celebration for everyone.

120425.explanation: The affordability of waste collection is under pressure, also in our municipality. The current policy addresses this; HELDER does not think this should be reversed.

120428.explanation: HELDER believes farmers deserve prospects in their own area. In consultation with agricultural organizations, HELDER wants to explore where red boundaries (building limits) can be expanded for housing. This requires customization, prioritizing landscape integration, sustainability, and spatial quality.

120429.explanation: HELDER believes everyone should be able to participate, regardless of income. Sports promote health, self-confidence, and social connection. Existing schemes (such as the Youth Fund for Sport & Culture or customized support via minimum income policies) can help people with welfare benefits.

120430.explanation: HELDER recognizes the value of a local broadcaster for information provision and oversight of power, but extra funding is not a given. The municipal contribution must match quality, reach, and relevance.

120436.explanation: HELDER considers safety a core government task. Residents must feel safe everywhere in the municipality. Community enforcement officers play an important role in livability and enforcement of local regulations. More deployment is desired, especially in neighborhoods with nuisance or unsafe situations.

120437.explanation: HELDER holds that safety must come first, especially in places where nuisance, vandalism, or criminal behavior occur. Camera surveillance can contribute to prevention, investigation, and a feeling of safety, provided it is used carefully and privacy is safeguarded.

120438.explanation: HELDER believes there must be sufficient shelter in every municipality for people fleeing domestic violence. If local provision is lacking or insufficient, the municipality must organize this shelter in cooperation with care and security regions.

120442.explanation: HELDER sees solving the housing shortage as a priority and is willing to think creatively. However, the tourist character of our municipality must not be overshadowed in this.

120443.explanation: HELDER believes in the value of contributing to society, even from a benefit situation. Socially meaningful work can contribute to self-confidence, social participation, and reintegration. This must always be appropriate and with attention to the personal situation of those involved.

120445.explanation: HELDER sees that an increasing group of people with lower middle incomes is getting into financial trouble. By raising the income threshold, these households can also receive help before falling into poverty. Prevention prevents bigger problems and increases livelihood security.

120447.explanation: HELDER wants to stimulate the transition to cleaner mobility. More charging points make electric driving more accessible to residents without a private driveway. Parking in general deserves extra attention to maintain a balance between livability and accessibility of the villages.

120448.explanation: HELDER considers traffic safety essential, especially for pedestrians, cyclists, and playing children. A maximum speed of 30 km/h within built-up areas significantly reduces the chance of serious accidents. Furthermore, it offers the opportunity to rethink our public space.

120449.explanation: HELDER notes that there has been a shortage in healthcare for years. Customization is always possible, but at one's own expense.

120451.explanation: HELDER wants to invest in good facilities, livability, and support for residents. Lowering property tax limits the municipality’s financial room and often mainly benefits wealthy homeowners. We focus on customized assistance for residents struggling to pay.

120454.explanation: HELDER supports strengthening the local economy, provided it occurs within the frameworks of sustainable development, spatial quality, and local employment. Space for entrepreneurs is necessary, provided it is carefully integrated into the landscape and aligned with regional needs.

120456.explanation: HELDER believes social rental homes should be fairly distributed among all housing seekers. We ensure that the implementation of legal policy is locally just, transparent, and explainable, so that other groups such as young people, starters, and urgent seekers also have prospects.

120459.explanation: HELDER believes, as a coastal municipality, it is important that children learn to swim well. However, school swimming has proven not to be the most effective method, partly because schools are not very motivated. HELDER pursues more flexible alternatives to promote swimming skills.

120461.explanation: HELDER wants to build according to need. Instead of following national percentages, HELDER looks at the local situation and housing demand. Noordwijk mainly needs housing for starters, middle incomes, and the elderly. We choose customization instead of normative thinking.

120462.explanation: HELDER allows expansion with taller residential flats, provided they fit well into the environment. What counts as tall depends on the location: the current standard of 4 residential floors does not have to be a limit if 5 or 6 floors are spatially responsible.

120464.explanation: HELDER opts for a slightly higher rate to make underground parking garages profitable and reclaim public space for greenery and housing. This keeps Noordwijk livable for residents and attractive for entrepreneurs and visitors. Currently, the costs are still passed on to the residents.

120466.explanation: HELDER believes every statutory task must be carried out. However, there is no concrete plan for safety, education, care, or guidance. There has also been no consultation with residents yet, while support for HELDER is a strict condition. As long as this basis is missing, implementation is not responsible.

120467.explanation: HELDER believes the bulb landscape is inseparably linked to the municipality's identity and is of great value for nature, recreation, and the economy. Building on bulb land is only possible if all other options have been investigated and ruled out.

120468.explanation: HELDER believes the seaside resort must remain well accessible for visitors, residents, and entrepreneurs. Therefore, we support extra parking spaces at the Boulevard and especially underground. This preserves space for greenery and terraces and prevents parking nuisance in residential areas.

120469.explanation: HELDER sees that the energy transition requires new and affordable choices. A regional heating network can help but only if it is reliable and remains affordable. Residents must not be locked into an expensive solution. Freedom of choice, transparency, and support are also paramount here.

120470.explanation: HELDER believes entrepreneurs should have space to run their business sustainably and profitably. Annual construction and dismantling is costly and burdensome for people and the environment. At the same time, year-round operations require clear conditions regarding safety, nature, and accessibility.

120417.explanation: It is up to the farmer how he or she utilizes the land and the harvest. We support nice local initiatives such as shopping at the farm and camping in the fields.

120419.explanation: Just like with the expansion of business parks, a distribution center can provide more local employment. In this case, careful consideration must be given to the location of the center and whether traffic to and from the municipality is not hindered.

120420.explanation: Buildings where seasonal workers stay remain empty for most of the year. This is poor use of the available housing. It would be better to have permanent residents live there. This would also help address the housing shortage.

120424.explanation: Now that a nationwide fireworks ban will be introduced, it is up to the municipality to ensure there is something to see in the sky on New Year’s Eve. If this is not arranged, people will certainly resort to illegal means, causing dangerous situations.

120425.explanation: Experience shows that complicated and paid systems for bulky waste lead to people dumping the waste on street corners. This causes nuisance and is almost impossible to enforce against. Ultimately, this waste is still removed by the municipality, which costs money instead of saving it.

120428.explanation: Like in the rest of the Netherlands, the housing shortage in the municipality of Noordwijk is urgent. Making land available from farmers is one of the many ideas that ONS Noordwijk supports to address this shortage.

120429.explanation: The opportunity to participate in sports should not depend on your income. If someone cannot afford access to a sports club, agreements should be made with the club (rather than directly funding the membership) to allow that person to play sports.

120430.explanation: Local broadcasters are the best source of information for residents to stay informed about municipal affairs. This reliable reporting must be preserved and the future of these broadcasters must be secured so that quality remains high.

120436.explanation: ONS Noordwijk supports the return of a real police station in the municipality. The gap between residents and law enforcement has become too large, and this is being felt.

120437.explanation: Camera surveillance is an ineffective measure that also infringes on our privacy. Instead, ONS supports bringing the police station back to the municipality. That will be a more preventive and effective way to combat crime.

120438.explanation: Everyone in the municipality deserves to feel safe. The municipality can play a role by establishing a shelter for domestic violence victims. There are many examples of how this has helped people in distressing situations get out of trouble.

120442.explanation: Like in the rest of the Netherlands, the housing shortage in the municipality of Noordwijk is urgent. Making holiday homes available is one of the many ideas that ONS Noordwijk supports to solve the shortage.

120443.explanation: Someone receiving social assistance should put as much energy as possible into finding (a higher) income. Mandatory unpaid community work has the opposite effect and causes people to remain stuck in their situation longer.

120445.explanation: Whether someone urgently needs poverty assistance depends on the personal situation. Income is not the only criterion to determine if someone is entitled to help from the municipality.

120447.explanation: The number of electric cars is expected to increase in the coming period. It is therefore more than logical that the number of available charging points grows accordingly.

120448.explanation: The maximum speed in a street should be assessed per situation. We are against a general speed reduction to 30 km/h. This should only be done if there is a dangerous situation caused by higher speeds.

120449.explanation: ONS Noordwijk is willing to help all residents, regardless of origin, religion, or beliefs. However, it is essential that this help is accepted. There is always a shortage of care personnel, and rejecting help based on culture is antisocial and improper.

120451.explanation: The property tax (ozb) is calculated based on the WOZ value of a home. This way, those with the greatest means bear the heaviest burden. Especially the ownership of a second home can be taxed more heavily.

120454.explanation: Larger business parks, including that of the European space institute, create increasing employment opportunities close to home. This results in shorter travel times and more prosperity within the municipality.

120456.explanation: If someone is a recognized refugee, they are considered by ONS to be part of Dutch society. When necessary, it is the municipality's task to help people find a first home. Alternatives like freeing up hotels are too expensive.

120459.explanation: It is essential for the safety of all residents in a coastal municipality that children learn to swim. The availability of swimming lessons should not depend on the parents' income. Cooperation with the rescue brigade is also possible for this.

120461.explanation: To retain young people and starters in the municipality, it is very important to invest extra in more social housing. Otherwise, they have no choice but to leave for other places. In the long term, this is disastrous for Noordwijk.

120462.explanation: Like in the rest of the Netherlands, the housing shortage in the municipality of Noordwijk is urgent. Allowing higher building is one of the many ideas that ONS Noordwijk supports to solve the shortage.

120464.explanation: Every resident of the municipality should be able to park for free throughout the entire municipality, not just in their own village center. For tourists, a more expensive parking spot will not reduce parking pressure at the seaside but will only cause jealousy.

120466.explanation: Now that the dispersal law remains in place, it is a matter of time before the national government imposes shelter obligations on the municipality. Compared to elsewhere in the country, the region accommodates relatively few asylum seekers. If no asylum center is established, it will be necessary to arrange shelter in hotels. Then costs will be much higher.

120467.explanation: Like in the rest of the Netherlands, the housing shortage in the municipality of Noordwijk is urgent. Making bulb fields available is one of the many ideas that ONS Noordwijk supports to solve the shortage.

120468.explanation: Like in Katwijk, a parking garage must be built in the dunes of Noordwijk. Only then can we truly reduce parking pressure. Residents and entrepreneurs have been indicating for a long time that there are too few parking spaces in Noordwijk, especially during the summer periods.

120469.explanation: To meet climate goals, we want to move away from gas. ONS wants to strongly focus on electrification instead of a heat network. Due to network congestion, the electrical infrastructure will have to be expanded anyway. It is more efficient to tackle this on a large scale than to also install a heat network.

120470.explanation: It is unfair that certain beach pavilions remain open in the winter months while others are forced to stay open. ONS wants to allow hospitality owners to decide themselves whether to open or not. The construction and dismantling every year costs entrepreneurs a lot.

statements.title: The municipality must prevent housing development on bulb-growing land.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.

titleParts.text: Werkgevers in de land- en tuinbouw moeten zelf zorgen voor de huisvesting van

titleParts.glossary: People who do work that only takes place during certain seasons, often in agriculture and fruit picking.

statements.title: The municipality should always allow farmers to open, for example, a shop or a campsite at their farm.
The municipality should pay the membership fees for a sports club for people receiving social assistance.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet het lidmaatschap van een sportclub betalen voor mensen met een

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

statements.title: The municipality must allow holiday homes to be used as a permanent residence.
There should be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Noordwijk who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: The municipality should support the local broadcaster with additional funding.

titleParts.glossary: Radio and television by and for people from the municipality and the immediate area.

statements.title: The municipality should organize a fireworks show on New Year's Eve.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.
For example cleaning the streets or keeping company and supporting elderly residents.

statements.title: Bulky waste must be collected from homes free of charge.
There should be a women's shelter for victims of domestic violence in Noordwijk.

titleParts.glossary: Shelter for women who experience violence from their partner or family. The shelter is secured; sometimes the location is secret.

statements.title: The property tax (onroerendezaakbelasting, OZB) should be reduced.

titleParts.glossary: Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.
Property tax. Tax you pay as the owner of a building or piece of land, or as the user of a commercial property.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
It should be possible for farmers to build houses themselves on their land.
Business parks in Noordwijk may be allowed to expand.
The municipality should raise the income threshold for receiving poverty assistance.
The municipality must provide more parking around the Boulevard.
The municipality should give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet vluchtelingen met een

titleParts.glossary: The right to reside in the Netherlands. The term can refer to the right itself or to the card that shows someone has that right.
A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: More high-rise apartment buildings may be allowed in Noordwijk.
The maximum speed limit in built-up areas should be set to 30 km/h.

titleParts.glossary: The maximum speed that cars and motorcycles are allowed to drive on the road.
An area with buildings (houses, other structures).

statements.title: All beach pavilions should be allowed to be open year-round.
The municipality must continue to provide funding for school swimming lessons.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet geld beschikbaar blijven stellen voor

titleParts.glossary: Swimming lessons organized by the school.

statements.title: People who need care should always be able to choose a caregiver who matches their religion, culture, or beliefs.

titleParts.text: Mensen die zorg nodig hebben, moeten altijd kunnen kiezen voor een zorgverlener die past bij hun geloof, cultuur of

titleParts.glossary: What someone believes about life, such as a religion or personal conviction.

statements.title: The municipality must cooperate with the establishment of a temporary asylum seekers center on Achterweg.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet meewerken aan de komst van een tijdelijk

titleParts.glossary: Reception center for asylum seekers. They live there until it is decided whether they may stay in the Netherlands. An asylum seekers’ center remains in a municipality for a long time.

statements.title: The municipality must oppose the establishment of new distribution centers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet de vestiging van nieuwe

titleParts.glossary: See distribution center.

statements.title: At least 35% of newly built homes in Noordwijk should be social rental housing. Currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social rental housing.

titleParts.text: Van de nieuw te bouwen woningen in Noordwijk moet minimaal 35% een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

titleParts.text: zijn. In Nederland is nu 28% van de woningen een

titleParts.glossary: A rental home with a low rent (in 2026 at most €932.93 per month) for people with a low income.

statements.title: There should be more camera surveillance in Noordwijk.

titleParts.glossary: Monitoring of a building, area, or (groups of) people using security cameras.

statements.title: Paid parking in Noordwijk aan Zee should be made more expensive.
A regional district heating network for the Bollenstreek should be created.

titleParts.glossary: Network of underground pipes that brings hot water from a central source to homes and buildings, for both heating and tap water.
